message.content:
Test file: src/claim-office.spec.ts
Implementation file: src/claim-office.ts (with src/cli.ts already written)
Failing test: "claim damage references item not in policy causes non-zero exit"
Current error: result.exitCode is 0 — runScenario doesn't throw when damage references an item type not in the referenced policy
Passing tests so far: 25

The test sets up a policy with only a sword, then a claim with damage to an amulet. Expected: runCli returns non-zero exitCode with stderr matching /not.*policy|amulet|not insured/i.

Modify `handleClaim` (or add validation) in `src/claim-office.ts` so that if a damage entry's `itemType` is not present in the policy's items, runScenario throws an Error with a message like "amulet not in policy" or similar.

The CLI already catches errors and returns { exitCode: 1, stderr: <message> }.

Keep the existing 25 tests passing. Don't refactor — just minimal change to make this test pass.

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, return when done.

content.text: I'll analyze the failing test and implement the minimal change needed.
Now I'll add minimal validation to `handleClaim` to throw an error when damage references an item type not in the policy.

content.text:
All 26 tests pass (was 25, now 25 + the newly activated test).

Green Phase Complete:
- Implementation: Added a minimal validation loop in `handleClaim` that throws `Error("<itemType> not in policy")` when a damage entry's `itemType` is not present in the referenced policy's items.
- Result: All tests now pass (26 passing, 2 todo).
- Approach: Minimal change — a simple `for...of` loop before the existing payout computation that throws on first missing item type. No refactoring of the existing payout logic; the validation is additive.

Relevant file: `/home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-16_23-33-33_claim-office-example-mapping_v7-hybrid-green-refactor_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/claim-office.ts`
